concurrency_vs_parallelism.title: Concurrency vs. Parallelism
concurrency_vs_parallelism.short_description: Concurrency means managing multiple tasks that take turns executing, while parallelism means tasks literally run simultaneously on multiple CPUs/cores. Understanding the difference is essential for choosing the right Python tool.

python_gil.title: The Python Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)
python_gil.short_description: The GIL is a mutex in CPython that allows only one thread to execute Python bytecode at a time. It simplifies memory management but limits true thread-based parallelism for CPU-bound tasks.
